scenario: Attacker executes a Base64-encoded PowerShell payload to evade command-line logging and string-based detection
why_tp: -Enc / -EncodedCommand flag — nearly always used to hide malicious payloads
-W hidden + -NonI + -NoP combination — standard attacker evasion chain
Parent process is cmd.exe — likely from a script or macro, not interactive user
Base64 decodes to New-Object System.Net.WebClient (download cradle)
analyst_action: Check if subsequent network connections were made from powershell.exe (DNS, HTTP)
Review what was downloaded/executed (check child processes of powershell.exe)
If confirmed malicious: isolate WORKSTATION-12 and review lateral movement
